text: 6 Minute English from bbclearningenglish .com
zh: [0] 6分钟英语，来自bbclearningenglish.com

text: Hello. This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: [1] 大家好，这里是BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Phil. And I'm Becca. Have you ever done something physically challenging, Phil?
zh: [2] 我是Phil。我是Becca。Phil，你做过什么很有身体挑战性的事情吗？

text: Like running a marathon, maybe? Or climbing a mountain?
zh: [3] 比如跑马拉松？或者爬山？

text: I've climbed some mountains, but only very very little ones, so it wasn't that challenging.
zh: [4] 我爬过一些山，不过都是非常非常小的山，所以算不上什么挑战。

text: If physical challenges are something you enjoy, maybe you should try artistic swimming, or synchronised swimming as it used to be called.
zh: [5] 如果你喜欢身体上的挑战，也许可以试试艺术游泳，也就是以前所说的花样游泳。

text: A combination of dance and gymnastics with a difference.
zh: [6] 它是舞蹈与体操的结合，但又有所不同。

text: In artistic swimming, athletes dance underwater while holding their breath.
zh: [7] 在艺术游泳中，运动员需要在水下屏住呼吸跳舞。

text: Did you say dance underwater? water? Yes, swimmers dance upside down in the water, holding their breath.
zh: [8] 你是说在水下跳舞？没错，游泳运动员会在水中倒立并屏住呼吸跳舞。

text: As we'll hear, it's an extremely demanding sport, which requires discipline, stamina and years of training.
zh: [9] 我们接下来会听到，这是一项极其考验人的运动，需要纪律性、耐力和多年的训练。

text: As usual, we'll be learning some useful new words and phrases, and you can practise these with the quiz and worksheet on our website.
zh: [10] 像往常一样，我们会学习一些实用的新单词和短语，你可以通过我们网站上的测验和练习题来巩固它们。

text: That's bbclearningenglish .com. Great.
zh: [11] 网址是bbclearningenglish.com。太好了。

text: But First, Phil, I have a question for you. Artistic swimming became an Olympic sport in 1984,
zh: [12] 不过Phil，我先问你一个问题。艺术游泳在1984年成为奥运会项目，

text: but which country has won the most gold medals since then?
zh: [13] 但自那以来哪个国家获得的金牌最多呢？

text: Is it… a Russia, b China, or c the USA?
zh: [14] 是a俄罗斯、b中国，还是c美国？

text: Ooh, I think it's b China. Well, we'll reveal the correct answer later in this episode.
zh: [15] 嗯，我觉得是b中国。好吧，我们会在节目后面揭晓正确答案。

text: Sylvia Solomoshyova from Slovakia is a European Championship medallist.
zh: [16] 来自斯洛伐克的Sylvia Solomoshyova是欧洲锦标赛的奖牌得主。

text: She's also the first artistic swimmer with over a million social media followers
zh: [17] 她也是第一位社交媒体粉丝超过百万的艺术游泳运动员，

text: after videos of her walking upside down under water went
zh: [18] 因为她在水下倒立行走的视频

text: viral. Here she explains the different types of swimming dances, called routines,
zh: [19] 走红了。在这里，她向Dushyane Navagayam解释了不同类型的游泳舞蹈动作，这些动作被称为“套路”，

text: to Dushyane Navagayam, presenter of BBC World Service programme, The Conversation.
zh: [20] Dushyane Navagayam是BBC世界频道节目《The Conversation》的主持人。

text: If you had to explain artistic swimming to someone who has never seen it, how would you describe it?
zh: [21] 如果要向从未看过艺术游泳的人解释这项运动，你会怎么描述？

text: So I would say it depends if you're watching like a solo or duet or team routine, because in solo there's one person, in duet there's two people.
zh: [22] 我想这取决于你看的是单人、双人还是团体项目，因为单人就是一个人，双人是两个人参与。

text: And in team routines there can be eight people or ten people.
zh: [23] 而团体项目可能有八到十个人。

text: And in the team routines there's a lot of lifts.
zh: [24] 在团体项目中会有很多托举动作。

text: And actually in the routines we are smiling,
zh: [25] 而实际上在表演过程中我们一直在微笑，

text: but we are hiding behind the smile because we are actually maybe dying.
zh: [26] 但我们其实是在笑容背后隐藏着痛苦，因为我们可能真的快“喘不过气”了。

text: We don't have oxygen, so… but we are smiling and people think that it's easy when it's actually not.
zh: [27] 我们没有氧气……但我们还是在笑，人们会以为这很轻松，其实完全不是。

text: Artistic swimming routines are performed solo,
zh: [28] 艺术游泳的套路可以是单人表演，

text: meaning by just one person, or as a duet, which means by two people.
zh: [29] 也就是只有一个人完成，或者是双人表演，也就是由两个人完成。

text: There are also team routines where one swimmer is lifted up out of the water.
zh: [30] 还有团体项目，其中一名选手会被托举出水面。

text: What all these routines have in common is that physically they are incredibly hard.
zh: [31] 所有这些项目的共同点是：从体力上讲，它们都极其艰难。

text: Yes, artistic routines are done upside down, underwater, without oxygen.
zh: [32] 是的，艺术游泳的动作是在水下倒立、且没有氧气的情况下完成的。

text: The swimmer's movements need to be as graceful as a ballet dancer.
zh: [33] 游泳运动员的动作需要像芭蕾舞者一样优雅。

text: And above all, it has to look effortless, because judges award points for artistic expression.
zh: [34] 而且最重要的是，动作必须看起来毫不费力，因为裁判会根据艺术表现力打分。

text: Silvia says she hides behind a smile – an idiom meaning to hide pain by presenting a happy outward appearance.
zh: [35] Sylvia说她“躲在微笑背后”——这是一个习语，意思是用快乐的外表掩饰内心的痛苦。

text: To succeed at the top level of any sport requires a huge amount of dedication.
zh: [36] 要在任何一项运动中达到顶尖水平，都需要极大的付出。

text: Sylvia trains eight hours a day, six days a week,
zh: [37] Sylvia每天训练八小时，每周训练六天，

text: under the guidance of her coach, who's also her mum, and a former artistic swimming champion herself.
zh: [38] 在她的教练——也是她母亲，同时也是前艺术游泳冠军——的指导下进行训练。

text: Here, Sylvia discusses the sacrifices she's made to succeed with Datiáni Nevenáigam for BBC World Service's The Conversation.
zh: [39] 在这里，Sylvia与BBC世界频道节目《The Conversation》的Datiáni Nevenáigam谈到了她为成功所做出的牺牲。

text: How easy was it being trained and coached by your mum?
zh: [40] 由妈妈来训练和指导你，是件容易的事吗？

text: I think it's good on one side because you can practice more at home
zh: [41] 我觉得一方面是好的，因为这样我在家也能多练习，

text: but on the other hand it's not sometimes good because you're training when you're at home when you're supposed to be like chilling.
zh: [42] 但另一方面有时也不太好，因为本该在家放松的时候，却还在训练。

text: There's no respite from it in a way I guess. Yeah I didn't have time for anything else.
zh: [43] 这样看来，某种程度上完全没有喘息的时间。是啊，我几乎没有时间做别的事情。

text: Sylvia thinks there are good and bad things about being coached by her mum.
zh: [44] Sylvia认为由妈妈来指导有好有坏。

text: It's good because she can practice at home but on the other hand it feels like she's training all the time.
zh: [45] 好处是她可以在家训练，但另一方面，这也让她感觉自己一直都在训练。

text: She uses the phrase on the other hand to show this contrasting opinion.
zh: [46] 她用“on the other hand”（另一方面）这个短语来表达这种对比的看法。

text: What Sylvia really looks forward to is chilling. A slang word meaning relaxing or hanging out.
zh: [47] Sylvia真正期待的是“chilling”，这是一个俚语，意思是放松或休闲。

text: For young athletes, balancing sport with school and friends is difficult, and it's hard to get a respite from training.
zh: [48] 对年轻运动员来说，平衡运动、学业和朋友关系很难，也很难从训练中获得喘息的机会。

text: A respite is a temporary break from doing something difficult.
zh: [49] respite（喘息）指的是从困难事务中获得的暂时休息。

text: Do you know what, Becca? I think I need a respite from all this talk talk about training.
zh: [50] Becca，你知道吗？我觉得我需要从这些关于训练的讨论中喘口气了。

text: Now, what was the answer to your question? Ah yes, Phil.
zh: [51] 那么，你那个问题的答案是什么呢？啊对了，Phil。

text: I asked you which country had won the most gold medals for artistic swimming since the beginning of the Olympic sport.
zh: [52] 我问你，自艺术游泳成为奥运项目以来，哪个国家获得的金牌最多。

text: And I said I thought it was China. Well, you're very close geographically. It is in fact Russia.
zh: [53] 我当时说我觉得是中国。你在地理位置上猜得很接近了，其实正确答案是俄罗斯。

text: OK, let's recap the vocabulary we've learnt, starting with solo – something done alone alone, and duet – something done by two people.
zh: [54] 好，我们来回顾一下今天学到的词汇，首先是solo——指独自完成的事情，还有duet——指由两个人完成的事情。

text: The idiom hide behind a smile means to hide painful feelings by presenting a happy external appearance.
zh: [55] 习语hide behind a smile的意思是用快乐的外表掩饰内心的痛苦。

text: The phrase, on the other hand, is used to introduce a contrasting opinion or statement.
zh: [56] 短语on the other hand用来引出一个相对的观点或说法。

text: Chillin' is slang for relaxing. And finally, a respite is a break from doing something difficult.
zh: [57] chillin'是俚语，意思是放松。最后，respite指从艰难的事情中获得的一段休息。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up, but if you'd like to practice the vocabulary you've learnt from this or any of our other episodes,
zh: [58] 我们的六分钟又到时间了，如果你想练习本期或其他节目中学到的词汇，

text: then head over to our website bbclearningenglish .com for quizzes, pronunciation tips and much more.
zh: [59] 欢迎访问我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com，那里有测验、发音技巧等更多内容。

text: Thanks for joining us and goodbye for now. Bye!
zh: [60] 感谢大家收听，我们下次再见。拜拜！
